nodes.x: Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it.
It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for the price thereof.
Take away the dross from the silver, and there shall come forth a vessel for the finer.
For the merchandise of it is better than the merchandise of silver, and the gain thereof than fine gold.

and are capable of superintending the concerns thereof. Or with princes that had gold, who filled their houses with silver:
Iron is taken out of the earth, and brass is molten out of the stone.

a temple of gold, of silver, or of brass? No, it will not. I am inclined to offer a chemical argument with regard to the material for building a temple in our present circumstances. The best materials, I have mentioned, probably. Iron might be better than stone; the time will come when the Lord will bring for brass gold, for iron silver, and for stones iron, and for wood brass, to beautify His…
Then set it empty upon the coals thereof, that the brass of it may be hot, and may burn, and that the filthiness of it may be molten in it, that the scum of it may be consumed.
And thy heaven that is over thy head shall be brass, and the earth that is under thee shall be iron.

Then was the iron, the clay, the brass, the silver, and the gold, broken to pieces together, and became like the chaff of the summer threshingfloors; and the wind carried them away, that no place was found for them: and the stone that smote the image became a great mountain, and filled the whole earth.
He setteth an end to darkness, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkness, and the shadow of death.
He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.

At that instant he looked to the Lord in prayer, and as he prayed darkness began to disperse from his mind and his soul was lit up as it was the evening before, and he' was filled with the Holy Spirit; and again did the Lord manifest his condescension and mercy: the heavens were opened and the glory of the Lord shone round about and rested upon him. While he thus stood gazing, and admiring, tho…
And thus the Lord caused stones to shine in darkness, to give light unto men, women, and children, that they might not cross the great waters in darkness.

Give glory to the Lord your God, before he cause darkness, and before your feet stumble upon the dark mountains, and, while ye look for light, he turn it into the shadow of death, and make it gross darkness.
A land of darkness, as darkness itself; and of the shadow of death, without any order, and where the light is as darkness.
The flood breaketh out from the inhabitant; even the waters forgotten of the foot: they are dried up, they are gone away from men.
As the waters fail from the sea, and the flood decayeth and drieth up:
He turned the sea into dry land: they went through the flood on foot: there did we rejoice in him.
Thou carriest them away as with a flood; they are as a sleep: in the morning they are like grass which groweth up.
Who is this that cometh up as a flood, whose waters are moved as the rivers?
Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.
As for the earth, out of it cometh bread: and under it is turned up as it were fire.

[Illustration: THE EARTH AS IT WOULD APPEAR IN COMPARISON WITH THE
FLAMES SHOOTING OUT FROM THE SUN.]
It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:
Therefore hath the curse devoured the earth, and they that dwell therein are desolate: therefore the inhabitants of the earth are burned, and few men left.
As soon then as they were come to land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread.
The stones of it are the place of sapphires: and it hath dust of gold.
And the second row, an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.
And the second row shall be an emerald, a sapphire, and a diamond.
And the foundations of the wall of the city were garnished with all manner of precious stones. The first foundation was jasper; the second, sapphire; the third, a chalcedony; the fourth, an emerald;
Then I looked, and, behold, in the firmament that was above the head of the cherubims there appeared over them as it were a sapphire stone, as the appearance of the likeness of a throne.
There is a path which no fowl knoweth, and which the vulture’s eye hath not seen:
Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the fowls of the air.

6. But behold lambs were borne by those white sheep, and they began to
open their eyes and to see, and to cry to the sheep. 7. Yea, they cried
to them, but they did not hearken to what they said to them, but were
exceedingly deaf, and their eyes were very exceedingly blinded. 8. And I
saw in the vision how the ravens flew upon those lambs, and took one of
those lambs, and dashed the sheep in…
And the Gods said: Let us prepare the waters to bring forth abundantly the moving creatures that have life; and the fowl, that they may fly above the earth in the open expanse of heaven.
The likeness of any beast that is on the earth, the likeness of any winged fowl that flieth in the air,
By them shall the fowls of the heaven have their habitation, which sing among the branches.
The lion’s whelps have not trodden it, nor the fierce lion passed by it.
The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the stout lion’s whelps are scattered abroad.
Where is the dwelling of the lions, and the feedingplace of the young lions, where the lion, even the old lion, walked, and the lion’s whelp, and none made them afraid?
A lion which is strongest among beasts, and turneth not away for any;
Will a lion roar in the forest, when he hath no prey? will a young lion cry out of his den, if he have taken nothing?
And say, What is thy mother? A lioness: she lay down among lions, she nourished her whelps among young lions.
He putteth forth his hand upon the rock; he overturneth the mountains by the roots.
His roots are wrapped about the heap, and seeth the place of stones.
In his hand are the deep places of the earth: the strength of the hills is his also.
Which removeth the mountains, and they know not: which overturneth them in his anger.

And he said, Go forth, and stand upon the mount before the Lord. And, behold, the Lord passed by, and a great and strong wind rent the mountains, and brake in pieces the rocks before the Lord; but the Lord was not in the wind: and after the wind an earthquake; but the Lord was not in the earthquake:
He looketh on the earth, and it trembleth: he toucheth the hills, and they smoke.
He cutteth out rivers among the rocks; and his eye seeth every precious thing.
For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;
For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his goings.
Behold, he cometh with clouds; and every eye shall see him, and they also which pierced him: and all kindreds of the earth shall wail because of him. Even so, Amen.
From the place of his habitation he looketh upon all the inhabitants of the earth.
He bindeth the floods from overflowing; and the thing that is hid bringeth he forth to light.
He bindeth the floods from overflowing; and the thing that is hid bringeth he forth to light.

When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens; and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth: he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.
He causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings for the rain; he bringeth the wind out of his treasuries.

When he uttereth his voice, there is a multitude of waters in the heavens, and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.
Behold, he spreadeth his light upon it, and covereth the bottom of the sea.
He sendeth out his word, and melteth them: he causeth his wind to blow, and the waters flow.
But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.
So shall the knowledge of wisdom be unto thy soul: when thou hast found it, then there shall be a reward, and thy expectation shall not be cut off.
Man knoweth not the price thereof; neither is it found in the land of the living.
Wherefore is there a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing he hath no heart to it?
For they are life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.
The depth saith, It is not in me: and the sea saith, It is not with me.
Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? or hast thou walked in the search of the depth?
Neither is it beyond the sea, that thou shouldest say, Who shall go over the sea for us, and bring it unto us, that we may hear it, and do it?
For thou hadst cast me into the deep, in the midst of the seas; and the floods compassed me about: all thy billows and thy waves passed over me.
Thus saith the Lord, which maketh a way in the sea, and a path in the mighty waters;
It cannot be gotten for gold, neither shall silver be weighed for the price thereof.
Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it.

Whatsoever administers to the sustenance, comfort and health of mankind
forms the basis of the commerce of the world. Gold and silver in coin
are only valuable as mediums to facilitate exchange. They can be made
useful to us and add to our comfort when made into cups, plates, etc.,
in our household economy. 10:227.
The gold and the crystal cannot equal it: and the exchange of it shall not be for jewels of fine gold.
It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.
It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, or the sapphire.
I will make a man more precious than fine gold; even a man than the golden wedge of Ophir.
The gold and the crystal cannot equal it: and the exchange of it shall not be for jewels of fine gold.
The stones of it are the place of sapphires: and it hath dust of gold.
No mention shall be made of coral, or of pearls: for the price of wisdom is above rubies.
For wisdom is better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.
There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
She is more precious than rubies: and all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared unto her.
The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it, neither shall it be valued with pure gold.
But he shall have power over the treasures of gold and of silver, and over all the precious things of Egypt: and the Libyans and the Ethiopians shall be at his steps.

all people; for the riches of eternity should be given to us, and it must needs be, saith the Lord, that the riches of the earth are mine to give. They are all His; how easily He could turn all the riches of the earth into our hands, if we were only prepared to receive them and use them according to His will. Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the heart?
Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living, and kept close from the fowls of the air.
There is a path which no fowl knoweth, and which the vulture’s eye hath not seen:
And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
Beasts, and all cattle; creeping things, and flying fowl:
Of fowls also of the air by sevens, the male and the female; to keep seed alive upon the face of all the earth.
Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
Destruction and death say, We have heard the fame thereof with our ears.
The ear that heareth the reproof of life abideth among the wise.
A sound of battle is in the land, and of great destruction.
A sound heart is the life of the flesh: but envy the rottenness of the bones.
Hear now this, O foolish people, and without understanding; which have eyes, and see not; which have ears, and hear not:
God understandeth the way thereof, and he knoweth the place thereof.
There is none that understandeth, there is none that seeketh after God.
What knowest thou, that we know not? what understandest thou, which is not in us?
Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.
Now, behold this is wisdom; whoso readeth, let him understand and receive also;
Therefore, whoso readeth it, let him understand, for the Spirit manifesteth truth;
For he looketh to the ends of the earth, and seeth under the whole heaven;
He directeth it under the whole heaven, and his lightning unto the ends of the earth.
Nevertheless we, according to his promise, look for new heavens and a new earth, wherein dwelleth righteousness.
The heaven shall reveal his iniquity; and the earth shall rise up against him.
And he saith unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.
To make the weight for the winds; and he weigheth the waters by measure.
For he commandeth, and raiseth the stormy wind, which lifteth up the waves thereof.
He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as an heap.

¶ Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and meted out heaven with the span, and comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?
Therefore his height was exalted above all the trees of the field, and his boughs were multiplied, and his branches became long because of the multitude of waters, when he shot forth.
When he made a decree for the rain, and a way for the lightning of the thunder:
Who hath divided a watercourse for the overflowing of waters, or a way for the lightning of thunder;

God has called by names of their own. He 
is God alone who made light out of darkness, 
and brought forth light from His treasures, 
and formed the chambers of the south wind,' and 
the treasure-houses of the deep, and the bounds 
of the seas, and the treasuries of snows and 
hail-storms, collecting the waters in the store- 
houses of the deep, and the darkness in His 
treasures, and bringing…
Fire, and hail; snow, and vapour; stormy wind fulfilling his word:
For he saith to the snow, Be thou on the earth; likewise to the small rain, and to the great rain of his strength.
Then did he see it, and declare it; he prepared it, yea, and searched it out.
Then I saw, and considered it well: I looked upon it, and received instruction.

"He thought for a moment, and then, evidently fancying that it would be
the safest plan to keep me out of the way of discovering anything for
the present, assented.
But obtained a promise that they should find it and see it in their flesh.
And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.
And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.
And wisdom and knowledge shall be the stability of thy times, and strength of salvation: the fear of the Lord is his treasure.
¶ The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
